name: content-research-writer-collaboration-framework

---
     1|# Feedback Templates & Citation Management
     2|
     3|## Section-by-Section Feedback Template
     4|
     5|When reviewing a section, use this structure:
     6|
     7|### What Works Well ✓
     8|
     9|List 2-3 specific strengths:
    10|
    11|- [Specific element] — Why it works well
    12|- [Specific element] — Why it works well
    13|- [Specific element] — Why it works well
    14|
    15|### Suggestions for Improvement
    16|
    17|#### Clarity
    18|
    19|- [Specific issue] → [Suggested fix or question to clarify]
    20|- [Complex sentence] → [Simpler alternative or rephrasing]
    21|
    22|#### Flow & Transitions
    23|
    24|- [Transition issue] → [Better connection]
    25|- [Paragraph order concern] → [Suggested reordering]
    26|
    27|#### Evidence & Support
    28|
    29|- [Claim needing citation] → [Type of evidence needed]
    30|- [Generic statement] → [How to make more specific]
    31|
    32|#### Style & Consistency
    33|
    34|- [Tone mismatch] → [Alignment needed]
    35|- [Word choice] → [Stronger alternative if relevant]
    36|
    37|### Specific Line Edits (If Applicable)
    38|
    39|**Original:**
    40|
    41|> [Exact quote from draft]
    42|
    43|**Suggested:**
    44|
    45|> [Improved version]
    46|
    47|**Why:** [Brief explanation]
    48|
    49|### Questions to Consider
    50|
    51|Ask 1-2 thought-provoking questions that deepen the section:
    52|
    53|- [Question that strengthens the thesis]
    54|- [Question that fills a gap]
    55|
    56|### Next Steps
    57|
    58|- Move to next section? OR
    59|- Revise based on feedback, then show revision? OR
    60|- Research specific gap before revising?
    61|
    62|---
    63|
    64|## Full Draft Review Template
    65|
    66|When reviewing a complete draft:
    67|
    68|### Overall Assessment
    69|
    70|**Strengths:**
    71|
    72|- [Major strength 1 with evidence]
    73|- [Major strength 2 with evidence]
    74|- [Major strength 3 with evidence]
    75|
    76|**Overall Impact:** [Your assessment of how well this will land with the target audience]
    77|
    78|### Structure & Flow
    79|
    80|- **Organization:** [Assessment of section order and logical progression]
    81|- **Transitions:** [Quality of connections between sections]
    82|- **Pacing:** [Does it feel rushed, balanced, or slow?]
    83|
    84|### Content Quality
    85|
    86|- **Argument Strength:** [Is the thesis clear and compelling?]
    87|- **Evidence Sufficiency:** [Do claims have adequate support?]
    88|- **Example Effectiveness:** [Do examples illuminate or distract?]
    89|
    90|### Technical Quality
    91|
    92|- **Grammar & Mechanics:** [Any consistent issues?]
    93|- **Citation Completeness:** [All claims sourced? Format consistent?]
    94|- **Consistency:** [Tone, terminology, style consistent throughout?]
    95|
    96|### Readability Assessment
    97|
    98|- **Clarity Score:** [1-5 scale; specific clear/unclear sections]
    99|- **Sentence Variety:** [Monotonous or dynamic?]
   100|- **Paragraph Length:** [Good pacing or heavy?]
   101|- **Jargon Level:** [Appropriate for audience?]
   102|
   103|### Section-by-Section Polish Suggestions
   104|
   105|1. **Introduction:** [Specific improvements for the hook/setup]
   106|2. **Section A:** [Strengths and one improvement]
   107|3. **Section B:** [Strengths and one improvement]
   108|4. **Section C:** [Strengths and one improvement]
   109|5. **Conclusion:** [Does it land? Any final thoughts?]
   110|
   111|### Title / Headline
   112|
   113|[Does current title work, or suggest alternatives?]
   114|
   115|### Pre-Publish Checklist
   116|
   117|- [ ] All factual claims have sources
   118|- [ ] All citations are complete and correct
   119|- [ ] Citation format is consistent throughout
   120|- [ ] Examples are clear and relevant
   121|- [ ] Transitions connect sections logically
   122|- [ ] Call-to-action or conclusion is present
   123|- [ ] No spelling/grammar issues
   124|- [ ] Tone matches audience
   125|- [ ] You approve the final version
   126|
   127|### Readiness Assessment
   128|
   129|**Ready to publish?** [Assessment and any final blockers]
   130|
   131|---
   132|
   133|## Citation Format Options
   134|
   135|### Inline Citations (Best for: Narrative, web, blogs)
   136|
   137|Maintains flow by integrating citations naturally:
   138|
   139|```markdown
   140|Studies show that remote teams are 20% more productive when given autonomy (McKinsey, 2024).
   141|
   142|Or: According to the McKinsey Global Institute's 2024 study, remote teams are 20% more productive when given autonomy.
   143|
   144|Or: Research indicates that autonomy drives productivity for remote teams [McKinsey Global Institute, 2024].
   145|```
   146|
   147|**Use when:** Story-driven or conversational writing; citations feel secondary to narrative
   148|
   149|### Numbered Citations (Best for: Academic, long-form, formal)
   150|
   151|Superscript numbers with comprehensive references at end:
   152|
   153|```markdown
   154|Remote teams show 20% higher productivity when given autonomy[1].
   155|
   156|---
   157|
   158|[1] McKinsey Global Institute. (2024). "The Future of Work: Remote Productivity and Autonomy." Retrieved from https://www.mckinsey.com/...
   159|```
   160|
   161|**Use when:** Academic standards, formal reports, or multiple detailed sources
   162|
   163|### Footnote/Endnote Citations (Best for: Formal documents, papers)
   164|
   165|Full citations in footer or end notes:
   166|
   167|```markdown
   168|Remote teams show 20% higher productivity when given autonomy.¹
   169|
   170|---
   171|
   172|¹ McKinsey Global Institute, "The Future of Work: Remote Productivity and Autonomy" (2024)
   173|```
   174|
   175|**Use when:** Scholarly work, formal documents, or complex citation needs
   176|
   177|---
   178|
   179|## Citation Consistency Audit Checklist
   180|
   181|When preparing final draft:
   182|
   183|**Format Consistency:**
   184|
   185|- [ ] All citations use the same style (all inline, all numbered, all footnote)
   186|- [ ] No mixed citation styles in same section
   187|- [ ] Citation placement is consistent (e.g., always before period or after)
   188|
   189|**Completeness:**
   190|
   191|- [ ] Every claim that needs a source has one
   192|- [ ] Every citation includes: Author, Year, Title (where applicable)
   193|- [ ] Every citation has publication source
   194|
   195|**Accuracy:**
   196|
   197|- [ ] I've verified each source exists and is credible
   198|- [ ] URLs (if included) are accurate and working
   199|- [ ] Author names and publication dates are correct
   200|
   201|**Format Details:**
   202|
   203|- [ ] Author names spelled consistently
   204|- [ ] Date format is consistent
   205|- [ ] Publication titles are formatted the same way
   206|- [ ] Punctuation around citations is uniform
   207|
   208|**References List (if applicable):**
   209|
   210|- [ ] All in-text citations are in the references list
   211|- [ ] References list is alphabetized
   212|- [ ] Format matches citation style throughout
   213|
   214|---
   215|
   216|## Collaboration Framework: Preserving Voice
   217|
   218|### Learning Your Style
   219|
   220|**Questions to ask early:**
   221|
   222|- Can you share a writing sample you love? What makes it yours?
   223|- What's your favorite writing? (Author, style, tone)
   224|- What writing puts you off?
   225|- How formal should this be?
   226|- Any phrases or words you always use?
   227|
   228|**Then:** Every suggestion I make matches your voice, not my preference.
   229|
   230|### During Feedback
   231|
   232|**Preserve voice by:**
   233|
   234|- Offering alternatives, not prescriptions
   235|- Suggesting edits that keep your word choices
   236|- Asking "Does this sound like you?" before finalizing suggestions
   237|- Learning your pace and rhythm; don't flatten them
   238|
   239|**Red flags:**
   240|
   241|- Your edits sound nothing like the original writer
   242|- You've replaced idiosyncratic language with generic alternatives
   243|- The final piece reads like corporate templates
   244|
   245|### Respecting Choices
   246|
   247|- If writer prefers their version over my suggestion: Support it
   248|- If writer rejects feedback: Don't revisit it in next section
   249|- If writer has a personal style preference: Adapt all feedback to match
   250|
   251|---
   252|
   253|**Bottom Line:** Your voice is the asset. I make it clearer and more credible, not different.
   254|
